task_completion.description: Visit official USDA websites to research crop market conditions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser open to an official USDA domain (e.g., usda.gov, ers.usda.gov, nass.usda.gov, wasde) showing market data for corn, soybeans, or wheat.

task_completion.description: Save an official USDA report or data file to the Downloads folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser download history showing a completed download from a USDA site, or a file manager window showing a USDA-related file (e.g., PDF, CSV, Excel) in the Downloads folder.

task_completion.description: Create the planting allocation brief at /home/ga/Desktop/planting_brief.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with the file saved as 'planting_brief.txt' on the Desktop, or the file icon visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Include recent price data for corn, soybeans, and wheat in the brief
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside 'planting_brief.txt' explicitly mentions 'corn', 'soybean' (or 'soybeans'/'soy'), and 'wheat' alongside numerical price figures (e.g., containing $, /bu, or per bu).

task_completion.description: Include USDA source citations in the brief
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside 'planting_brief.txt' references official USDA sources (e.g., ERS, NASS, WASDE, or specific USDA URLs).

task_completion.description: Provide a planting recommendation based on the data
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside 'planting_brief.txt' contains a concluding section or statement recommending a specific planting allocation or strategy for the upcoming season.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to access USDA data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GUI web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) navigating USDA websites, rather than terminal commands fetching data blindly.

integrity.description: The downloaded file appears to be a genuine report from the web
integrity.visual_evidence: The file in the Downloads folder has a realistic name and extension (e.g., .pdf, .xlsx) consistent with USDA reports, not a manually created dummy text file.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI text editor to write the brief
integrity.visual_evidence: The brief is seen being edited in a graphical text editor (e.g., Mousepad, Gedit, VS Code) rather than being generated entirely via terminal echo/cat commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The brief must be saved exactly at /home/ga/Desktop/planting_brief.txt. It must include data for 'corn', 'wheat', and 'soybean' (or 'soybeans'/'soy'). The prices should be formatted with indicators like $/unit, /bu, or per bu. The research must come from official USDA domains (usda.gov, ers.usda.gov, nass.usda.gov, marketnews.usda.gov, fas.usda.gov).
